citations.description: Implementation of the partial-baseline no-suppression logic
Equivalence suite — 5 tests, none testing absent-baseline field = local-wins via the canonical path
Heldout equivalence suite — 4 tests, none testing absent-baseline field = local-wins via the canonical path
test_none_baseline_local_wins uses old _diff_fields from adapters.jira, not the new canonical path

findings.criterion: A field absent from the canonicalized baseline is treated as "no ancestor" (per-field local-wins), matching today's whole-None-baseline arbitration — verified by a characterization case in the equivalence suite.
findings.detail: The implementation in `outbound_field_diff.py` line 251 correctly returns `False` (no suppression) when a field is absent from the canonical baseline (`if field not in _INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS or field not in baseline: return False`), preserving per-field local-wins. However, neither `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_canonical_outbound_equivalence.py` (5 tests, ends at line 174) nor `test_canonical_outbound_equivalence_heldout.py` (4 tests, ends at line 148) contains any test case exercising the new canonical path with a partial/absent-field baseline to verify this behavior. The existing `test_none_baseline_local_wins` in `test_baseline_arbitration.py` uses the OLD adapter-internal `_diff_fields` (imported as `from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira import outbound_fields as _of`), not the new canonical `compute_update_fields` / `diff_canonical_fields` path. The AC explicitly requires the proof to appear in the equivalence suite.
findings.remediation: Add a test to `test_canonical_outbound_equivalence.py` (or `_heldout.py`) that calls `compute_outbound_mutations` with a binding store whose `get_baseline` returns a PARTIAL canonical baseline dict (i.e. a dict that omits some inbound-mirrored fields), and asserts that the absent-from-baseline fields are still emitted (local-wins) even when the remote matches the local value — proving the 'field not in baseline → no suppression' branch of `diff_canonical_fields`.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
